questions.stem: Which clause of the 14th Amendment has been most frequently used by the Supreme Court to apply the Bill of Rights to state governments?
options: The Privileges or Immunities Clause
The Due Process Clause
The Equal Protection Clause
The Citizenship Clause

questions.stem: In Dobbs v. Jackson Women's Health Organization (2022), the Supreme Court held that the Constitution does not confer a right to abortion. Which of the following best describes the constitutional reasoning used by the majority?
options: The right to abortion violates the Establishment Clause of the First Amendment
The right to abortion is not deeply rooted in the nation's history and tradition, and therefore is not protected by the Due Process Clause
The Commerce Clause does not grant Congress the power to regulate medical procedures
The Tenth Amendment reserves all healthcare decisions exclusively to the states

questions.stem: A state legislature passes a law requiring all public school students to begin each day with a moment of silent prayer. Under the Lemon test established in Lemon v. Kurtzman (1971), which of the following would MOST likely be the basis for a court striking down this law?
options: The law lacks a secular legislative purpose
The law imposes an undue burden on interstate commerce
The law violates the Second Amendment right to bear arms
The law discriminates on the basis of race in violation of the Equal Protection Clause

questions.stem: What is the primary function of the elastic clause (Necessary and Proper Clause) in Article I, Section 8 of the Constitution?
options: It grants the president emergency powers during wartime
It allows Congress to pass laws needed to carry out its enumerated powers
It permits the Supreme Court to declare laws unconstitutional
It gives states the right to nullify federal legislation

questions.stem: Which of the following scenarios BEST illustrates the concept of selective incorporation?
options: Congress passes a law that applies differently in each state based on population
The Supreme Court rules that the Second Amendment right to bear arms applies to state governments through the 14th Amendment
A state court interprets its own constitution to provide broader protections than the federal Constitution
The president issues an executive order that supersedes a state law

questions.stem: A political scientist studying voter turnout finds that states with same-day voter registration have turnout rates approximately 5-7 percentage points higher than states requiring advance registration. Based on this finding and your knowledge of political participation, which of the following conclusions is MOST supported?
options: Same-day registration causes higher voter turnout in all demographic groups equally
Reducing institutional barriers to registration is associated with increased political participation
States with same-day registration have more educated populations, which explains the higher turnout
Advance registration requirements are unconstitutional under the 15th Amendment
